itemListElement.name: AJ20D6 / AJ300D (3.0L Straight-Six Twin-Turbo Diesel MHEV)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Jaguar F-Pace D300 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned D300 3.0-litre Ingenium diesel engine typically costs £5,500–£8,500 supply only. Used engines are available from £3,500–£5,500. Rebuilt engines start from approximately £6,500. Fitting adds £1,300–£2,000 depending on your location and workshop. Main dealer new engines can exceed £14,000.

mainEntity.name: Is the Jaguar F-Pace D300 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The D300 is widely regarded as the most reliable F-Pace diesel option. The 3.0-litre straight-six Ingenium is significantly more robust than the 2.0-litre diesel, with fewer major failure patterns. However, it has known issues: timing chain stretch (oil dilution from DPF regeneration is a contributing factor), DPF clogging (urban use), and EGR cooler failures. With proper maintenance, the D300 is a durable and rewarding engine.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Jaguar F-Pace D300?

acceptedAnswer.text: The most frequently reported issues are timing chain stretch (accelerated by oil dilution from DPF regeneration), DPF clogging (short urban journeys), and EGR cooler failures. The 3.0-litre is less prone to these issues than the 2.0-litre, but vigilance is still required.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Jaguar F-Pace D300 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value. Against a well-kept £40,000 F-Pace D300, a £7,000 recon is 17.5% — definitely worth it. Against a high-mileage £20,000 example, the same recon approaches our 60% Jaguar threshold — get a scrap valuation first.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Jaguar F-Pace D300 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The D300 carries the engine code AJ20D6 (also referred to as AJ300D in some documentation). It's a 3.0-litre straight-six twin-turbo diesel with 48V mild-hybrid assistance, used in the F-Pace X761 from 2021 onwards.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the timing chain, or does it need full engine replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A preventative timing chain and tensioner replacement costs £1,200–£2,500. Oil dilution from failed DPF regenerations is a key contributor to timing chain wear. If ignored and the chain snaps, the engine is an interference design — pistons and valves collide, resulting in catastrophic engine damage. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately. Regular oil changes (10,000-mile intervals) are the single most effective preventative measure.

replacementCosts.h2: Jaguar F-Pace D300 Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the AJ20D6 3.0-litre Ingenium diesel engine. Rebuilt engines are available from approximately £6,500. Main dealer new engines can exceed £14,000.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an F-Pace D300 engine typically takes 14–18 hours at £75–£110/hr (independent) or £130–£180/hr (London/SE dealer).

cta.label: → Compare D300 engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Timing Chain Stretch & Oil Dilution
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise on cold start, metallic ticking from the engine, engine warning light, DPF filling prematurely, oil level rising on the dipstick.
problems.repairCost: £1,200–£2,500 (chain + tensioner replacement)【PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet】
problems.replacementCost: £5,500–£9,500 (if chain snaps and pistons strike valves)

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattle, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: Oil dilution from failed DPF regenerations is a key contributor to timing chain wear on Ingenium diesel engines. Stretched timing chains can cause the DPF to fill prematurely, creating a cascade of failures. The 3.0-litre is less prone to this than the 2.0-litre, but vigilance is still required. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, get the chain and tensioners inspected immediately. Regular oil changes using the correct 0W-20 JLR-spec oil are essential.

failureLink.label: Read the full timing chain failure guide

problems.title: DPF Clogging & Regeneration Issues
problems.symptoms: DPF warning light, reduced performance, increased fuel consumption, failed regeneration cycles, oil dilution.
problems.repairCost: £300–£900 (forced regeneration, DPF clean)【PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet】
problems.recommendation: The Ingenium diesel's DPF is sensitive to short urban journeys. The MHEV system helps but doesn't eliminate the risk. If you predominantly drive in town, ensure the vehicle completes regular regeneration cycles — a motorway run at 2,000+ rpm for 20 minutes at least once a week is recommended. The 3.0-litre is less prone to these issues than the 2.0-litre, but they remain a known pattern.

failureLink.label: Read the full DPF failure guide

problems.title: EGR Cooler Failures
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss with no visible external leak, white exhaust smoke, engine management light, overheating.
problems.repairCost: £500–£1,000 (EGR cooler replacement)【PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet】
problems.recommendation: EGR cooler failure is a well-documented issue across the Ingenium diesel family. If you're experiencing unexplained coolant loss, have the EGR cooler inspected promptly. Ignoring it can lead to overheating and potential engine damage, though it doesn't typically progress to full engine replacement.

failureLink.label: Read the full EGR cooler failure guide

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: F-Pace D300 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Preventative — catches the fault before catastrophic failure
Forced regeneration or DPF clean — less than replacement
Specialist repair available; cheaper than engine replacement
Typically requires full engine replacement or complete rebuild

buyingChecks.title: Buying This D300? Check These First
buyIf: * Full Jaguar service history with evidence of regular oil changes using 0W-20 JLR-spec oil — ideally every 10,000 miles
* Evidence of DPF-friendly driving — regular motorway runs
* No cold-start rattle — a quiet startup suggests healthy timing chain
* Post-2021 build with the revised Ingenium diesel
avoidIf: * No proof of regular oil changes — the Ingenium diesel is unforgiving of neglect
* DPF warning light with no regeneration history
* Cold-start rattle with no timing chain inspection
* Engine warning light with timing, EGR, or DPF codes and no diagnosis

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The D300 is the pick of the F-Pace diesel range — a smooth, powerful straight-six that represents JLR's most refined diesel engine. It's significantly more robust than the 2.0-litre diesel, with fewer reported issues. But it's still a modern diesel with modern diesel problems — timing chain vigilance, DPF maintenance, and EGR cooler attention are the price of entry. Buy on documented history, not on faith. A well-maintained D300 is a £35,000–£55,000 premium SUV; a neglected one is a £7,000 engine replacement waiting to happen.

cta.label: → Compare D300 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Jaguar F-Pace D300 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling or metallic ticking (timing chain stretch warning)
* Service History — including the D300 era (2021–present) and confirmed oil-change intervals using 0W-20 JLR-spec oil — ideally every 10,000 miles
* DPF Check — Check for DPF warning light or evidence of regeneration issues (urban-driven vehicles are high risk)
* Oil Level — Check the dipstick; rising oil level indicates fuel dilution from failed DPF regenerations
* Smoke Check — White smoke on startup can indicate EGR coolant leak
* Coolant Level — Any history of coolant loss without visible leaks? (EGR cooler warning)
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved timing, EGR, DPF, or cooling system issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
